title: 29-yr-old woman dies in office washroom after boss repeatedly denies sick leaves in S Africa
excerpt: A 29-year-old woman collapsed and died in washroom of her office after she was denied sick leaves in South Africa. Gcina Dhladhla, who was struggling with exhaustion, allegedly informed her supervisors several times that she was feeling unwell. Her family said she submitted two medical certificates during weeks preceding the incident, but her requests for sick leaves were denied.

title: China put secret tracking device on UK PM's car in 2022
excerpt: A Chinese tracking device was discovered on UK PM's official car, Charles Parton, a member of think-tank Council on Geostrategy, told Commons Business and Trade Committee. "PM's car in 2022 was emanating data to China through the cellular module," Parton said. However, it is nor clear who out of Boris Johnson, Liz Truss or Rishi Sunak was being targeted.

title: Boat carrying 35 pilgrims capsizes in Godavari river in Maha; 2 killed
excerpt: Two women drowned and several others were injured after a boat carrying over 35 pilgrims capsized in Godavari River in Maharashtra's Beed, officials said. The boat was heading to Purushottampuri, shrine dedicated to Lord Purushottam, which has seen a large number of devotees during the holy month of 'Adhik Maas'. "It...lost balance due to uneven weight distribution," an eyewitness said.

title: Money should keep coming: Dushyant Kukreja on online criticism
excerpt: On Curly Tales' Tere Gully Mein, Dushyant Kukreja talked about online criticism. He said that he refuses to give it too much space in his head. As long as he’s entertaining people, earning, receiving positive feedback, and staying within those boundaries, he sees little reason to let anonymous comments dictate how he creates.
